mnemonics: CRUD: Create, Retrieve, Update, Delete - the core functions an IR system must handle for its index.
keyPoints: An IR system's design is centered on building an efficient index (inverted index) for fast retrieval.
The ranking of results is typically determined by a ranking algorithm (e.g., BM25, Vector Space Model).
Evaluation of an IR system is done using metrics like Precision, Recall, and F-Measure.
commonMistakes: Confusing Information Retrieval (finding documents) with Data Retrieval (fetching precise database records).
Overlooking the importance of stop word removal and stemming in the indexing process, which hurts efficiency.
